fedEvents.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
fedEvents.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

fedEvents.title: Trump administration's limits on student loan forgiveness program are blocked. What to know
fedEvents.content: Trump's policy that narrowed eligibility for a popular student loan forgiveness program will not go into effect, after two federal judges blocked the rule.

items.title: OpenAI reportedly considers handing 5% stake to the government. Here’s why the AI lab would consider that move.
items.content: Bringing the government on board as an equity partner may serve to dilute some of the criticism of the AI industry that has developed momentum in recent months.

items.title: Protein-packed Cheerios and cat food: How General Mills plans to combat a tough spending backdrop
items.content: “Cat growth is on fire,” one executive said.

items.title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to reckon with first.
items.content: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

items.title: Nvidia is betting on a trillion-dollar robotics boom. Here is the hidden way to trade it.
items.content: CEO Jensen Huang has called humanoid robots a “multitrillion-dollar economic opportunity.”

items.title: ‘We were stunned’: My daughter, 39, said her mother-in-law gives her more money than we do. Do I call her out?
items.content: “She thanked us for the $100 birthday gift and then announced that her mother-in-law had sent her $400.”

items.title: CoreWeave, Nebius shares tumble as Meta stands to become a fresh threat in the cloud
items.content: Meta’s reported interest in monetizing its AI infrastructure is leading investors to question the sustainability of neocloud business models.

items.title: This is the best time ‘in a generation’ to buy space and defense stocks, analysts say
items.content: Wedbush analysts are taking a bullish view of the  space sector, initiating coverage of SpaceX and other stocks.

items.title: Where can I invest my kid’s ‘Trump account’ money? The Treasury Department just answered that question.
items.content: The money in “Trump accounts” has to be invested in low-cost index funds — and now parents and investors are learning which funds they can actually use.

items.title: ServiceNow and Salesforce shares now look like buys, as ‘Armageddon’ fears are too extreme, analyst says
items.content: A Guggenheim analyst says that valuations for the two software stocks are too depressed, even though the AI threat is real.

items.title: Sandisk’s and Micron’s stocks sink as the rotation trade builds, but supply shortages should limit losses
items.content: Most of Sandisk’s annual revenue could eventually come from its new business model contracts that provide better visibility, BofA said.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.content: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.content: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans

items.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
items.content: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

items.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
items.content: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

items.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
items.content: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

items.content: Companies with good payouts are now the hottest bet in China markets.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Fall But Direction for Long-End Yields Still Seen Upward
items.content: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield fell and ING said the the long end of the Treasury curve will continue trading at higher yields even as Trump hasn’t delivered anything to shock markets so far.

items.title: Russia's Crude Exports Fairly Stable Despite U.S. Sanctions, Goldman Says
items.content: Oil edged lower in early trade as Goldman Sachs said the latest round of U.S. sanctions against Russia haven’t had a significant impact on the country’s oil exports.

items.title: The Extra Reward for Owning Stocks Over Bonds Has Disappeared

items.title: Canada to Review Amazon Contracts After Quebec Layoffs

items.title: What Happens Now That TikTok's Gone Dark?
items.content: The company told users the app isn’t available and told them to stay tuned. Trump said he will likely intervene.

items.content: Oura’s smart ring measures heart health and can predict when you may be getting sick

items.title: Google loses fight over record $4.7 billion EU antitrust fine

items.content: Shares of Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plummeted more than 7% in early Thursday trading.

items.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: report
items.content: Trump said in June that the U.S. taking an ownership stake in AI giants would be "a beautiful thing" and make American public "partners in this revolution."

items.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
items.content: An historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

items.title: Volkswagen braces for boardroom showdown over historic cost-cutting plan
items.content: Volkswagen is poised for a boardroom showdown following reports that the auto giant is weighing up shutting four factories and implementing 100,000 job cuts.

items.title: Amazon is designing its own AI chips for Echo, Fire TV and future devices, exec tells CNBC
items.content: Amazon hardware chief Panos Panay says the company is designing custom chips for key devices as it experiments with AI gadgets.

items.title: Oil prices on pace for fourth straight weekly loss as Trump sees progress in U.S.-Iran talks
items.content: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.

items.title: Tank maker KNDS postpones IPO amid market struggles for defense
items.content: Shareholders of the Amsterdam-based company have decided the process to IPO will proceed "upon the return of more favorable market conditions," KNDS said.

items.title: Apple plans five new iPhones through 2027, eyes Chinese-made chips amid foldable push, reports say
items.content: Apple is planning five new iPhones by 2027 and is considering Chinese memory suppliers as it expands foldable production, reports say.

items.title: U.S. won’t renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations with Canada and Mexico
items.content: President Donald Trump's "primary" issue with USMCA centers on America's trade deficits with Canada and Mexico, a senior administration official said.

items.title: M&A is ‘on fire’ as large-cap firms simplify, Citi UK CEO says
items.content: Citi U.K. CEO Tiina Lee said U.K. M&A is “on fire” as companies simplify and overseas buyers target British assets.

items.title: Meta says WhatsApp usernames are safeguarded against scams after India flags cybersecurity risks

items.title: Alibaba-affiliate Ant Group rushes into humanoid robots with a dozen deals in 18 months
items.content: Ant has led a 500 million yuan ($73.59 million) funding round in humanoid robotics company Zeroth, the start-up announced Thursday.

items.title:
Inside India newsletter: Amazon, Walmart-owned Flipkart get ready to shake up
India's delivery-in-minutes sector
items.content: Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart are scrambling to expand quick commerce operations in India, not just to chase growth but to remain relevant to consumers.

items.title: South Korean government discriminated against Coupang, U.S. companies, House report finds

items.title: Chip stocks that notched record rallies in second quarter start Q3 with a dud

items.title: Prolific tech analyst Dan Ives is exiting Wedbush for a new venture

items.title: Trump says outside funds 'run my money' after disclosure shows billions in 2025 revenue

items.title: Automakers report mixed U.S. sales results as hybrid vehicles drive market
items.content: Second-quarter U.S. new vehicle sales show a sharp divide, with automakers offering hybrid models largely outperforming those without  them.

items.title: Jim Cramer says to use Wednesday's market rotation to your advantage. Here's how he'd play it
items.content: CNBC’s Jim Cramer said investors should use the latest market rotation to buy some of the biggest winners they may have missed.

items.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates
